scenario: Wagtail work session - audit every production subsystem that depends on the page model before reworking its contract

map_quality.rationale: The answer correctly identifies Wagtail as a Django CMS, enumerates the key package split (models, admin, contrib, api, search), and pins the Page model to `wagtail/models/pages.py:289` with the AbstractPage base and MP_Node tree mechanism named — enough for small targeted follow-ups. It does not describe the serve path or explain how admin/API/contrib all hang off the same tree node, so the agent still needs to discover coupling breadth.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line citations (`/wagtail/models/pages.py:289`, AbstractPage at line 248), named classes (Page, AbstractPage, MP_Node), and per-package paths with real contrib examples — the agent can navigate directly without a secondary discovery pass.

justification.rationale: The answer locates the model and names its tree mechanism but does not argue why Page is the central contract being reworked — there is no statement that every serving view, admin surface, API endpoint, and contrib app chains through this single node, which is the reason the restructuring is risky.

uncertainty.rationale: No caveats at all: line numbers are asserted without flagging that they may shift, and the package list is presented as complete with no note about what might be missing (e.g., wagtail/snippets, wagtail/images, wagtail/documents).

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps core fields with file:line and covers tree mechanics (MP_Node path/depth/numchild) and alias_of, but the live/draft publishing state — the most restructuring-sensitive part — is completely absent: no `live`, `has_unpublished_changes`, `go_live_at`, or `expire_at` fields. The `.specific` property (content_type-based downcast) is implied by the content_type FK but never explained, and PageQuerySet helpers are cited only as a vague blob at 'line 6039+' with no concrete method names.

specificity.rationale: Fields are pinned to individual line numbers (e.g., `alias_of` at line 392, `content_type` at line 306), which is good; but MP_Node tree fields (path, depth, numchild) have no file:line at all, PageQuerySet methods are unlisted, and key serving/routing methods (`serve`, `route`, `get_url`) are absent entirely.

justification.rationale: The answer does not say which pieces of the contract other code leans on most — it never notes that `live` is queried by virtually every listing view, that `content_type` underpins every `.specific` downcast, or that `alias_of` introduces a shadow-page pattern that requires special-casing; the only dependency hint is the generic 'User queries via Page.objects.all()'.

uncertainty.rationale: Every line number is stated as fact with no hedging, despite the live/draft and locale fields being outright missing — the agent cannot tell whether those omissions are intentional or oversights; 'line 6039+' is the only admitted imprecision, and no gaps are flagged as unverified.

steps.step: Enumerate every production dependent of the page model

map_quality.rationale: The three dependents the audience rubric calls out as the hardest to find — the site root FK (wagtail/models/sites.py), search promotions, and template tags — are all absent; the answer covers admin views, API, and contrib forms but misses these scattered production pieces entirely. Several entries also collapse multiple call sites into a single ':1' line (e.g., 'listing.py:1 — Multiple views') rather than listing each site separately, so the flat-list goal is only partially met.

specificity.rationale: A significant fraction of entries use ':1' as the line number (e.g., wagtail/search/models.py:1, contrib/redirects/models.py:1, contrib/sitemaps/models.py:1) which is not a pinned call site but just 'start of file'; several entries explicitly note 'Multiple views' which is the collapse the rubric forbids. Entries with real line numbers (home.py:124, home.py:241, chooser.py:259) are actionable, but they are the minority.

justification.rationale: The step asks for production code paths only, but the list explicitly includes test files: wagtail/users/tests/test_admin_views.py:2024, wagtail/api/v2/tests/test_pages.py, wagtail/contrib/forms/tests/test_views.py, wagtail/contrib/redirects/tests/test_redirects.py, wagtail/tests/test_reference_index.py, and wagtail/tests/test_audit_log.py:157 — six test-file entries pollute the production list without flagging.

uncertainty.rationale: No part of the answer flags what it could not fully verify or where blind spots might remain; the three explicitly audience-flagged categories (site root FK, search promotions, template tags) are silently absent rather than noted as unverified, so the agent has no signal to follow up on the gaps.

map_quality.rationale: The answer produces 16 subsystems with coupling kinds stated, but omits several the rubric explicitly names: no 'Serving' subsystem (the actual page serve view), no 'Sites' subsystem (the root_page FK merits its own bucket, not a one-liner under URL Routing), no 'Reports' subsystem (aging pages, locked pages reports), and no 'Template tags' subsystem; the chooser and dashboard panels are also absent, meaning the agent still needs exploratory sweeps of those areas.

specificity.rationale: Many entries carry plausible-looking line numbers (e.g. :2147, :2188, :52) but a recurring pattern of ':1' anchors (redirects/models.py:1, sitemaps/models.py:1, sites.py:1) signals synthetic placeholders rather than verified locations, and several entries resolve only to directories or package paths ('/wagtail/search/backends/', '/wagtail/api/v2/serializers/') with no file or line at all.

justification.rationale: Each subsystem includes a 'Dependency:' line naming the coupling kind (ForeignKey, queryset, field access), which does convey the shape of the change; however no subsystem is distinguished as higher-risk than another, so a teammate cannot read priority or restructuring order from the grouping alone.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags nothing as unverified despite multiple ':1' placeholder lines, directory-level references, and entire subsystems (reports, template tags, serving) that may be missing; the complete absence of hedging will cause a downstream agent to treat uncertain anchors as authoritative.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer names driving flows per dependent and lists ForeignKey vs GenericForeignKey vs query-only coupling, but the primary organizing axis is user-facing impact tier rather than the schema-embedding-vs-transient-querier split the rubric requires; search promotions (explicitly named in the rubric as a canonical schema-embedding dependent) are entirely absent, which is a significant map gap.

specificity.rationale: Several entries carry concrete line numbers (pages.py:499-533, :2147, :2188, :2629, :2669, forms/models.py:45) but others use wildcards ('/wagtail/admin/views/pages/*.py') or directory-level paths ('/wagtail/search/', '/wagtail/api/v2/') with no line anchor, meaning the agent would need follow-up reads for those entries.

justification.rationale: Coupling kinds are labeled (ForeignKey, GenericForeignKey, field name, query), and editor/visitor flows are named, but the risk ranking is mostly asserted ('Impact: Site completely broken') rather than argued from the causal chain between coupling tightness and blast severity — the answer doesn't explain why a FK-holding dependent ranks above a transient querier.

uncertainty.rationale: No judgment calls are flagged, no gaps acknowledged, and no 'this list may be incomplete' caveat is present, even though search promotions are missing and several paths lack line-level verification — the answer reads as exhaustive when it is not.

map_quality.rationale: The rubric calls out the canonical hard-to-find dependents — lazy string FKs in sites.py and search_promotions that never import Page, the multiline-import dashboard panel, and the template tag — none of which appear in the answer; instead the answer surfaces moderately non-obvious but structurally standard FK/GenericFK references (many inside pages.py itself), which a diligent text search would not miss.

specificity.rationale: Most entries carry a concrete file:line (e.g., /wagtail/contrib/redirects/signal_handlers.py:180, /wagtail/contrib/forms/models.py:45), but entry 7 collapses to a glob pattern ('/wagtail/admin/rich_text/converters/*.py:174, 236') rather than naming the specific file, and three entries point back into pages.py itself, not scattered dependents.

justification.rationale: The 'why overlooked' explanations are impressionistic ('feels separate', 'appears to be just a manager') rather than faithful to the actual evasion mechanism; none identifies the key reasons the rubric cites — e.g., that a lazy string FK referencing 'wagtail.models.Page' as a string never shows up in an import grep, or that a template tag registers a reference through a decorator chain invisible to naive search.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer makes no admission of blind spots or areas it could not verify; it presents 10 items with uniform confidence, leaving the agent no signal about where the list might be incomplete.

map_quality.rationale: The map spans 16 named subsystems with per-dependent what-to-check notes, and the JSON block explicitly carries over the earlier step lists; however, test files are included (test_admin_views.py:2024, test_pages.py:25) despite the rubric's 'stay on production dependents' instruction, the search backends entry resolves to a directory path not a file, and template tags and search promotions — called out by name in the rubric — appear only implicitly or not at all.

specificity.rationale: Roughly half the entries carry real line numbers (edit.py:52, home.py:124, chooser.py:259, move_page.py:41/63/97) while the other half use ':1' placeholders (redirects/models.py:1, sitemaps/models.py:1, search/models.py:1, i18n.py:1, audit_log.py:1, simple_translation/models.py:1, page_utils.py:1, page_chooser_panel.py:1); a PR author landing on those entries must still locate the actual site before acting.

justification.rationale: Most per-entry checks follow their coupling kind (url_path → 'url_path concatenation works', path/depth → permission checker checks, ParentalKey → 'ParentalKey to Page works'), and the field-dependency summary ties each field to named dependents; but several checks collapse to a generic 'Page.objects queries work' that doesn't distinguish between a transient queryset caller and a schema-embedding FK, which matters most for ordering the restructuring.

uncertainty.rationale: The only uncertainty flag is a generic end-of-document caveat ('NOT exhaustive — starting point; new dependents may emerge') rather than inline markers on the ':1' entries or missing subsystems; the model does not flag which line numbers it could not verify, leaving the agent unable to distinguish 'confirmed at this line' from 'file-level approximation'.
